response:
Yes we can check how long it took for udp packet to be sent to server like ping but only one direction

To do this we can use a timer and measure the time between sending the packet and receiving the response

We can also use the System.Diagnostics.Stopwatch class to measure the elapsed time

We can then compare the time taken to send the packet with the time taken to receive the response

This will give us the round trip time for the udp packet

We can also use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient class to send and receive udp packets

We can then measure the time taken for each operation

This will give us the time taken for the udp packet to be sent and received

We can also use the System.Net.NetworkInformation.Ping class to measure the time taken for the packet to be sent

This will give us the one way time for the udp packet

We can then compare the time taken for the packet to be sent with the time taken for the packet to be received

This will give us the round trip time for the udp packet

We can also use the System.Net.NetworkInformation.Ping class to measure the time taken for the packet to be sent

This will give us the one way time for the udp packet

We can then compare the time taken for the packet to be sent with the time taken for the packet to be received

This will give us the round trip time for the udp packet
